hazard.summary: Validated actor identity is ignored across the authentication-to-rate-limiter bucket-selection boundary, causing shared proxy IP bucket selection and false rate-limit denial for authenticated users.
signals: Authenticated users bucketed with shared proxy IPs.

constraints: Rate-limit enforcement must scope capacity buckets to the authenticated actor when authentication is valid.
Limiter classification must not prefer shared proxy IP over validated user identity for authenticated requests.
Authenticated users must not be coupled into the same rate-limit bucket solely because they share a proxy IP.
Fallback to IP-based rate limiting must apply only when the request is anonymous or the authentication token is invalid.
Rate-limit bucket selection must preserve the actor signal across the authentication-to-limiter boundary.
When trusted client IP is unavailable, anonymous fallback buckets must be scoped to endpoint or artifact, not globally shared.
verification: Setup: Configure two authenticated users behind the same proxy IP with valid tokens and a small rate-limit capacity per bucket. Action: Exhaust capacity with user A, then send a request with user B. Assertion: User B is evaluated against a distinct per-user bucket and is not denied because user A exhausted the shared proxy IP bucket.

Setup: Send an authenticated request with a valid token from a proxy IP shared by other users. Action: Inspect or trace the limiter bucket key selected for the request. Assertion: The selected bucket key uses the authenticated user identity and does not use only the shared proxy IP.

Setup: Send anonymous and invalid-token requests from the same proxy IP used by authenticated users. Action: Inspect or trace the limiter bucket key selected for each request. Assertion: IP fallback is used only for anonymous or invalid-token traffic, not for valid authenticated traffic.

Setup: Configure a test where a shared proxy IP bucket is already exhausted by unauthenticated traffic. Action: Send a valid authenticated request from the same proxy IP. Assertion: The authenticated request is classified into its per-user bucket and is not rejected due to the exhausted anonymous/IP bucket.

Setup: Remove trusted client IP headers so the limiter cannot see the real client IP. Action: Send anonymous download requests from two different clients. Assertion: Each client is bucketed by endpoint/artifact scope, not by a single global ip:unknown bucket.
notes: Dominant mechanism: valid authentication identifies the consuming actor, but rate-limit bucket selection ignores the actor signal at the classification boundary and selects shared proxy IP capacity, causing false denial for authenticated users. Three distinct fixes landed: (1) PR #412 — auth-aware limiter uses per-user bucket for authenticated requests, IP fallback only for anonymous/invalid-token. (2) PR #421 — relative Retry-After + enriched 429 CLI error message. (3) Commit 9e09581 — when trusted client IP unavailable, anonymous downloads fall back to endpoint/artifact-scoped bucket instead of global ip:unknown. SirBrenton filed diagnosis comment Mar 4 identifying rate-limit UX collapse and auth-bucket misattribution.

constraints: Rate-limit bucket selection must preserve validated actor identity.
Authenticated capacity accounting must be isolated per authenticated actor.
IP fallback must not override valid authentication.
Anonymous fallback buckets must not be globally shared across unrelated actors or artifacts.
